title: Executive Order No. 547, s. 1952
text:
MALACAÑANG MANILA

EXECUTIVE ORDER NO. 547

FIXING THE CEILING PRICES OF COMMODITIES AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES

By virtue of the powers vested in me by section 3 of Republic Act No. 509, entitled “An Act declaring national policy, authorizing the President of the Philippines for a limited period to fix ceiling prices of commodities and to promulgate rules and regulations regarding prices of commodities to effectuate such policy, and authorizing the appropriation of a certain sum for the purpose,” and upon the recommendation of the Price Administration Board, I, Elpidio Quirino, President of the Philippines do hereby order:

SECTION 1. The following essential commodities shall not be sold at more than the maximum selling prices for importers, wholesalers and retailers set opposite each:

FOODSTUFF (LOCAL)

FOODSTUFF (IMPORTED)

Without 17 per cent Special Excise Tax on Foreign Exchange

SEC. 2. The ceiling prices fixed in this Order include for Cafe Puro the 7 per cent sales tax and for Hereford Brand Corned Beef the 7 per cent sales tax and 1 per cent municipal tax.

SEC 3. This Order shall take effect immediately.

Done in the City of Manila, this 11th day of November, in the year of Our Lord, nineteen hundred and fifty-two, and of the Independence of the Philippines, the seventh.

(Sgd.) ELPIDIO QUIRINO President of the Philippines

By the President:

(Sgd.) MARCIANO ROQUE Acting Executive Secretary

Source : Presidential Museum and Library

Office of the President of the Philippines. (1952). [Executive Order Nos. : 488 – 664]. Manila : Presidential Museum and Library.
